source: Mordheim core rulebook, Part 3 - Campaigns & Optional Rules (Hired Swords / Dramatis Personae sections), fetched from https://www.broheim.net/downloads/rules/Mordheim%20-%20Part%203%20-%20Campaigns%20%26%20Optional%20Rules.pdf on 2026-07-03. Owner should cross-check against their own physical rulebook printing/errata. List is NOT exhaustive — only the Hired Swords whose full entries were captured from the fetched pages (106-109) are included. TODO: verify vs rulebook p.105-110 for any remaining Hired Swords (the section runs to at least p.110) before treating this as complete.

hiredSwords.name: Pit Fighter
hiredSwords.mayBeHiredBy: Any warband apart from Undead and Skaven.
hiredSwords.ratingBonus: +22 points, plus 1 point per Experience point he has.
hiredSwords.equipment: Morning star, spiked gauntlet (counts as an additional hand weapon and a buckler — Heroes cannot learn to use the gauntlet itself) and helmet.

hiredSwords.name: Ogre Bodyguard
hiredSwords.mayBeHiredBy: Any warband except Skaven. Ogre (Maneaters) warbands may hire an Ogre Bodyguard even though they otherwise cannot hire Hired Swords (see the Cannibals special rule).
hiredSwords.ratingBonus: +25 points, plus 1 point per Experience point he has.
hiredSwords.equipment: Either two swords, axes or clubs (any mix), or a double-handed weapon (hiring player chooses). Wears light armour.
hiredSwords.specialRules: Fear: causes fear. Large Target: counts as a Large Target for shooting purposes.

hiredSwords.name: Halfling Scout
hiredSwords.mayBeHiredBy: Any warband except Skaven, Undead and the Possessed.
hiredSwords.ratingBonus: +5 points, plus 1 point per Experience point he has.
hiredSwords.equipment: Bow, dagger, and a cooking pot (counts as a helmet).
hiredSwords.specialRules: Cook: a warband with a Halfling Scout may increase its maximum warband size by +1 (does not increase the maximum number of Heroes).

hiredSwords.name: Warlock
hiredSwords.mayBeHiredBy: Any warband except Witch Hunters and Sisters of Sigmar.
hiredSwords.ratingBonus: +16 points, plus 1 point per Experience point he has.
hiredSwords.specialRules: Wizard: has two spells generated at random from the Lesser Magic list. When gaining a new skill, may choose Academic skills or randomly determine a new Lesser Magic spell instead.

hiredSwords.name: Freelancer
hiredSwords.ratingBonus: +21 points, plus 1 point per Experience point he has.
hiredSwords.equipment: Heavy armour, shield, lance and sword. Optionally rides a warhorse (M8 WS3 BS0 S3 T3 W1 I3 A1 Ld5) if using the mounted-models optional rules; mounted armour save 3+, on foot 4+.

hiredSwords.name: Elf Ranger
hiredSwords.mayBeHiredBy: Mercenaries and Witch Hunters. Warbands that include Dwarfs may still hire an Elf Ranger, but pay 40 gc upkeep instead of 20.
hiredSwords.ratingBonus: +12 points, plus 1 point per Experience point he has.
hiredSwords.equipment: Elf bow, sword and Elven cloak.
hiredSwords.specialRules: Seeker: when rolling on the Exploration chart, may modify one dice by -1/+1. Excellent Sight: spots Hidden enemies at twice his Initiative value in inches. Elven skills (may choose instead of normal skills once earned through experience, not possessed at recruitment): Fey (hostile spells fail on the Elf on a D6 roll of 4+); Luck (once per game may re-roll any one of his own dice rolls).

hiredSwords.name: Dwarf Troll Slayer
hiredSwords.mayBeHiredBy: Mercenaries and Witch Hunters. Warbands that include Elves may still hire a Troll Slayer, but pay 20 gc upkeep instead of 10.
hiredSwords.ratingBonus: +12 points, plus 1 point per Experience point he has.
hiredSwords.equipment: Two axes or a double-handed axe (hiring player chooses).
hiredSwords.specialRules: Deathwish: immune to all psychology and never needs to test for fighting alone. Hard to Kill: only taken out of action on an Injury roll of 6 (instead of 5-6); treat a 5 as stunned. Hard Head: ignores special rules for maces, clubs, etc. Troll Slayer skills (may choose instead of normal skills): Ferocious Charge (double Attacks when charging, -1 to hit that turn); Monster Slayer (always wounds on 4+ regardless of Toughness, unless his own Strength would need a lower roll); Berserker (+1 to hit when charging).
